step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the conclusion that goblet cells are responsible for proliferation along the length of the villi. One additional point supporting this view is their role not only in movement but also in secreting mucus, which aids in protecting and lubricating the gastrointestinal tract as they migrate up through the epithelial layer. 

**Answer:** B: Goblet cells

This reinforces the importance of goblet cell behavior in maintaining gut health and functionality across the entire villus surface.
